client.h(3) Library Functions Manual client.h(3)

NAME

client.h - ACIP client-side protocol API.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t acip_client_receive_and_dispatch (acip_transport_t *transport, const acip_client_callbacks_t *callbacks)
Receive packet from server and dispatch to callbacks. asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame (acip_transport_t *transport, const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t pixel_format)
Send image frame to server (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame_h265 (acip_transport_t *transport, h265_encoder_t *encoder, const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)
Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_join (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t capabilities)
Announce client join to server (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_leave (acip_transport_t *transport)
Notify server of client leaving (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_start (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t stream_types)
Request to start media streaming (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_stop (acip_transport_t *transport, uint8_t stream_types)
Request to stop media streaming (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_capabilities (acip_transport_t *transport, const void *cap_data, size_t cap_len)
Send terminal capabilities to server (client → server). asciichat_error_t acip_send_protocol_version (acip_transport_t *transport, const protocol_version_packet_t *version)
Send protocol version to server (client → server).

Detailed Description

ACIP client-side protocol API.

Client-side API for ascii-chat protocol. Provides functions for:

  • Receiving and dispatching packets from server
  • Sending image frames to server
  • Sending client control messages (join, leave, stream control)
  • Sending capabilities and protocol version

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file include/ascii-chat/network/acip/client.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t acip_client_receive_and_dispatch (acip_transport_t * transport, const acip_client_callbacks_t * callbacks)

Receive packet from server and dispatch to callbacks. High-level receive function for client side. Receives a single packet from the transport, handles decryption, and dispatches to appropriate callback handler.

Parameters

transport Transport instance
callbacks Callback structure for handling received packets

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Definition at line 30 of file lib/network/acip/client.c.

31                                                                                              {
32   if (!transport || !callbacks) {
33     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ INVALID_PARAMS: transport=%p, callbacks=%p", (void *)transport, (void *)callbacks);
34     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or callbacks");
35   }
36 
37   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 📥 RECV_START: transport=%p, checking connection state", (void *)transport);
38 
39   // Check if transport is connected
40   if (!transport->methods->is_connected(transport)) {
41     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ NOT_CONNECTED: transport not ready for receive");
42     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport not connected");
43   }
44 
45   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ CONNECTED: transport ready, checking transport type");
46 
47   packet_envelope_t envelope;
48   bool enforce_encryption = (transport->crypto_ctx != NULL);
49   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🔐 CRYPTO_MODE: enforce_encryption=%s", enforce_encryption ? "yes" : "no");
50 
51   socket_t sock = transport->methods->get_socket(transport);
52   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🔌 TRANSPORT_SOCKET: socket=%d (INVALID=%d)", sock, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
53 
54   void *packet_data = NULL;
55   void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
56   size_t packet_len = 0;
57 
58   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 📥 RECV_CALL: using transport->methods->recv()");
59   asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
60   log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 📥 RECV_RESULT: error=%d, packet_len=%zu, data=%p, alloc=%p", recv_result, packet_len,
61            packet_data, allocated_buffer);
62 
63   if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
64     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ RECV_FAILED: error code %d", recv_result);
65     return recv_result;
66   }
67 
68   // Parse packet header
69   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] HEADER_CHECK: packet_len=%zu, header_size=%zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
70   if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
71     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ HEADER_TOO_SMALL: %zu < %zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
72     buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
73     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Packet too small: %zu < %zu", packet_len, sizeof(packet_header_t));
74   }
75 
76   const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)packet_data;
77   envelope.type = NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
78   envelope.len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
79   envelope.data = (uint8_t *)packet_data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
80   envelope.allocated_buffer = allocated_buffer;
81   envelope.allocated_size = packet_len;
82   envelope.was_encrypted = false;
83 
84   log_info("[ACIP_RECV] HEADER_PARSED: type=%u (0x%04x), len=%u, total_size=%zu", envelope.type, envelope.type,
85            envelope.len, packet_len);
86 
87   log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "ACIP received packet: type=%u, len=%u, total_size=%zu", envelope.type,
88                 envelope.len, packet_len);
89 
90   // Validate packet length
91   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] LENGTH_VALIDATION: payload_size=%u vs actual=%zu", envelope.len,
92             packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
93   if (envelope.len != packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
94     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ LENGTH_MISMATCH: header=%u, actual=%zu", envelope.len,
95               packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
96     buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
97     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Packet length mismatch: header says %u, actual %zu", envelope.len,
98                      packet_len - sizeof(packet_header_t));
99   }
100 
101   // Handle PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED from server (decrypt and extract inner packet)
102   if (envelope.type == PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED) {
103     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🔐 ENCRYPTED_PACKET: decrypting PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED (len=%u)", envelope.len);
104     asciichat_error_t decrypt_result = packet_decrypt_envelope(&envelope, transport->crypto_ctx);
105     if (decrypt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106       log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ DECRYPT_FAILED: %d", decrypt_result);
107       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
108       return decrypt_result;
109     }
110   }
111 
112   // Dispatch packet to appropriate ACIP handler
113   log_info("[ACIP_RECV] 🎯 DISPATCH_START: type=%u (0x%04x), data_len=%u, callbacks=%p", envelope.type, envelope.type,
114            envelope.len, (void *)callbacks);
115   asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
116       acip_handle_client_packet(transport, envelope.type, envelope.data, envelope.len, callbacks);
117 
118   if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
119     log_error("[ACIP_RECV] ❌ DISPATCH_FAILED: type=%u, error=%d", envelope.type, dispatch_result);
120   } else {
121     log_info("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ DISPATCH_OK: type=%u (0x%04x) handled by callbacks", envelope.type, envelope.type);
122   }
123 
124   // Always free the allocated buffer (even if handler failed)
125   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] 🗑️  CLEANUP: freeing buffer %p (size=%zu)", envelope.allocated_buffer,
126             envelope.allocated_size);
127   if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
128     buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
129   }
130 
131   log_debug("[ACIP_RECV] ✅ RECV_COMPLETE: type=%u, result=%d", envelope.type,
132             dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? -1 : 0);
133 
134   // Return handler result
135   return dispatch_result;
136 }




References acip_handle_client_packet(),
    packet_envelope_t::allocated_buffer,
    packet_envelope_t::allocated_size, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    buffer_pool_free(), acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    packet_envelope_t::data, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED,
    acip_transport_methods_t::get_socket, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    acip_transport_methods_t::is_connected,
    packet_envelope_t::len, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_error, log_info,
    acip_transport::methods, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, packet_decrypt_envelope(),
    PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED, SET_ERRNO,
    packet_envelope_t::type, packet_header_t::type,
    US_PER_MS_INT, and packet_envelope_t::was_encrypted.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_capabilities
  (acip_transport_t * transport, const void * cap_data, size_t
  cap_len)


Send terminal capabilities to server (client → server).
    Reports client's terminal capabilities (color support, dimensions, etc.)




Parameters


transport Transport instance


cap_data Capability data buffer


cap_len Capability data length


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 300 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


300                                                                                                             {
301   if (!transport || !cap_data) {
302     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or cap_data");
303   }
304 
305   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES, cap_data, cap_len, 0);
306 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES,
    and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_join
  (acip_transport_t * transport, uint8_t capabilities)


Announce client join to server (client → server). Notifies
    server that client is ready to participate in session.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


capabilities Client capability bitmask (video, audio, etc.)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 262 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


262                                                                                            {
263   if (!transport) {
264     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport");
265   }
266 
267   // Simple capability byte payload
268   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_JOIN, &capabilities, sizeof(capabilities), 0);
269 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_JOIN, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_client_leave
  (acip_transport_t * transport)


Notify server of client leaving (client → server). Clean
    disconnect notification to server.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 271 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


271                                                                       {
272   if (!transport) {
273     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport");
274   }
275 
276   // Leave has no payload
277   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_LEAVE, NULL, 0, 0);
278 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_LEAVE, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame
  (acip_transport_t * transport, const void * pixel_data, uint32_t
  width, uint32_t height, uint32_t pixel_format)


Send image frame to server (client → server). Sends webcam
    video frame as RGB24 pixel data.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


pixel_data RGB24 pixel data (width * height * 3 bytes)


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


pixel_format Pixel format (e.g., RGB24)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 142 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


143                                                                                 {
144   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Called with %ux%u, transport=%p, pixel_data=%p", width, height, (void *)transport,
145             pixel_data);
146 
147   if (!transport || !pixel_data) {
148     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Invalid params");
149     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or pixel_data");
150   }
151 
152   // Calculate pixel data size (3 bytes per pixel for RGB24)
153   size_t pixel_size = (size_t)width * height * 3;
154   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: pixel_size=%zu", pixel_size);
155 
156   // Create image frame packet header
157   image_frame_packet_t header;
158   header.width = HOST_TO_NET_U32(width);
159   header.height = HOST_TO_NET_U32(height);
160   header.pixel_format = HOST_TO_NET_U32(pixel_format);
161   header.compressed_size = 0;
162   header.checksum = 0;
163   header.timestamp = 0;
164 
165   // Calculate total size
166   size_t total_size;
167   if (checked_size_add(sizeof(header), pixel_size, &total_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
168     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Overflow in size calculation");
169     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Packet size overflow");
170   }
171 
172   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: total_size=%zu, allocating buffer", total_size);
173 
174   // Allocate buffer
175   uint8_t *buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
176   if (!buffer) {
177     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: Failed to allocate %zu bytes", total_size);
178     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer: %zu bytes", total_size);
179   }
180 
181   // Build packet
182   memcpy(buffer, &header, sizeof(header));
183   memcpy(buffer + sizeof(header), pixel_data, pixel_size);
184 
185   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: About to send packet");
186   // Send via transport
187   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME, buffer, total_size, 0);
188 
189   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME: packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
190 
191   buffer_pool_free(NULL, buffer, total_size);
192   return result;
193 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_pool_alloc(),
    buffer_pool_free(), image_frame_packet_t::checksum,
    image_frame_packet_t::compressed_size, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED,
    image_frame_packet_t::height, HOST_TO_NET_U32,
    log_debug, packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME, image_frame_packet_t::pixel_format,
    SET_ERRNO, image_frame_packet_t::timestamp, and
    image_frame_packet_t::width.


Referenced by threaded_send_image_frame().






asciichat_error_t acip_send_image_frame_h265
  (acip_transport_t * transport, h265_encoder_t * encoder, const
  void * pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (client → server).
    Encodes RGB frame to H.265 and transmits as PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265.
    Much more efficient than raw pixel transmission for bandwidth-limited
    connections.




PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height:
  u16][h265_data...]


    
  
  • flags: H.265 encoding flags (keyframe, size_change)
    • 
  
  • width: Frame width in pixels
    • 
  
  • height: Frame height in pixels
    • 
  
  • h265_data: x265-encoded frame payload
    • 



Parameters


transport Transport instance


encoder H.265 encoder (initialized with initial frame dimensions)


pixel_data RGB pixel data from camera/source


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Encoder is responsible for RGB → YUV420 conversion
  internally



Encoder handles frame dimension changes transparently
    (reconfigures on size change)




Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (client →
  server).




Encodes frame using H.265 codec and sends as
    PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265. Packet format:
    [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_encoded_data...]




Parameters


transport ACIP transport


encoder H.265 encoder instance


pixel_data RGB pixel data from capture


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


Error code


Definition at line 208 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


209                                                                                                       {
210   if (!transport || !encoder || !pixel_data) {
211     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Invalid params");
212     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport, encoder, or pixel_data");
213   }
214 
215   // Allocate output buffer for H.265 encoding
216   // H.265 can produce output larger than input due to codec overhead
217   // Use 2x multiplier to account for worst-case frame content
218   size_t output_buffer_size = 5 + (width * height * 2);
219   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_1] About to allocate output buffer (%zu bytes)", output_buffer_size);
220   uint8_t *output_buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, output_buffer_size);
221   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_2] ✅ Allocated output buffer");
222   if (!output_buffer) {
223     log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Failed to allocate output buffer");
224     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate H.265 output buffer: %zu bytes", output_buffer_size);
225   }
226 
227   // Encode frame
228   size_t encoded_size = output_buffer_size - 5;
229   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_3] Calling h265_encode");
230   asciichat_error_t encode_result = h265_encode(encoder, (uint16_t)width, (uint16_t)height, (const uint8_t *)pixel_data,
231                                                 output_buffer + 5, &encoded_size);
232   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_4] h265_encode returned: %d, encoded_size=%zu", encode_result, encoded_size);
233 
234   if (encode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
235     log_error("H.265 encoding failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(encode_result));
236     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
237     return encode_result;
238   }
239 
240   // Prepare H.265 packet: [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
241   output_buffer[0] = 0; // flags: no flags set by default
242   output_buffer[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
243   output_buffer[2] = width & 0xFF;
244   output_buffer[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
245   output_buffer[4] = height & 0xFF;
246 
247   size_t total_size = 5 + encoded_size;
248 
249   log_debug("★ ACIP_SEND_IMAGE_FRAME_H265: Encoded %ux%u → %zu bytes", width, height, total_size);
250 
251   // Send via transport
252   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_5] Calling packet_send_via_transport with %zu bytes", total_size);
253   asciichat_error_t result =
254       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265, output_buffer, total_size, 0);
255   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_6] packet_send_via_transport returned: %d", result);
256 
257   buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
258   log_dev("[ACIP_SEND_H265_7] ✅ Freed output buffer, returning from acip_send_image_frame_h265");
259   return result;
260 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_pool_alloc(),
    buffer_pool_free(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    h265_encode(), log_debug, log_dev, log_error,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_protocol_version
  (acip_transport_t * transport, const protocol_version_packet_t *
  version)


Send protocol version to server (client → server).
    Negotiates protocol version with server.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


version Protocol version structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 308 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


308                                                                                                                     {
309   if (!transport || !version) {
310     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport or version");
311   }
312 
313   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, version, sizeof(*version), 0);
314 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_start
  (acip_transport_t * transport, uint8_t stream_types)


Request to start media streaming (client → server).
    Notifies server that client wants to start sending video/audio.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


stream_types Stream type bitmask (video, audio, etc.)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 280 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


280                                                                                             {
281   if (!transport) {
282     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport");
283   }
284 
285   // Server expects uint32_t (4 bytes), not uint8_t
286   uint32_t stream_types_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)stream_types);
287   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_STREAM_START, &stream_types_net, sizeof(stream_types_net), 0);
288 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, HOST_TO_NET_U32,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_STREAM_START, and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t acip_send_stream_stop
  (acip_transport_t * transport, uint8_t stream_types)


Request to stop media streaming (client → server). Notifies
    server that client wants to stop sending video/audio.




Parameters


transport Transport instance


stream_types Stream type bitmask (video, audio, etc.)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 290 of file
    lib/network/acip/client.c.


290                                                                                            {
291   if (!transport) {
292     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid transport");
293   }
294 
295   // Server expects uint32_t (4 bytes), not uint8_t
296   uint32_t stream_types_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)stream_types);
297   return packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_STREAM_STOP, &stream_types_net, sizeof(stream_types_net), 0);

    Version 0.11.0
    ascii-chat
